core.app.name: PymeSec
core.dashboard.screen.title: Workspace Dashboard
core.dashboard.screen.subtitle: A quick audit-oriented view of the current organization context and work queues.
core.management-reporting.screen.title: Management Reporting
core.management-reporting.screen.subtitl: Cross-domain reporting across assessments, evidence, risks, and findings for the active workspace context.
core.support.screen.title: Support and Concepts
core.support.screen.subtitle: A practical guide and concept reference for the areas used across the application.
core.governance.screen.title: Delegated Access Governance
core.governance.screen.subtitle: Separate workspace accountability and object visibility governance from platform-only administration.
core.shell.title: Compliance Workspace
core.shell.subtitle: Manage compliance work across the organization.
core.shell.workspace_copy: Manage the selected area for the active organization and scope.
core.shell.no_selection: No visible menu with a route is currently selected.
core.shell.theme_copy: Themes change the look and feel of the workspace without changing how the application works.
core.shell.theme_rule_core: Use approved themes to keep the interface consistent.
core.shell.theme_rule_plugins: All screens should follow the shared visual language of the workspace.
core.shell.theme_rule_tokens: Theme switching stays safe because only approved visual tokens change.
core.shell.debug_copy: Detailed technical information lives here instead of the main workspace.
core.shell.debug_footer: Use this panel to inspect technical details without mixing them into the main workspace.
core.shell.error.title: Screen unavailable
core.shell.error.unavailable_subtitle: The requested area cannot be opened in the current workspace context.
core.shell.error.unavailable_message: The requested section [:menu] is not available for the current person, organization, or access level.
core.shell.error.unimplemented_subtitle: The section exists, but its page is not available yet.
core.shell.error.unimplemented_message: The requested section [:menu] has not been prepared yet.
core.platform.screen.title: Platform Overview
core.platform.screen.subtitle: Application administration, system posture, and recent administrative activity in one place.
core.plugins.screen.title: Installed Modules
core.plugins.screen.subtitle: Available modules, activation state, and dependency status across the application.
core.permissions.screen.title: Permission Catalog
core.permissions.screen.subtitle: Access rules, their origin, and where each one applies.
core.permissions.summary: This catalog stays read-only and explains which permissions exist, where they come from, and in which contexts they apply.
core.permissions.table.label: Label
core.roles.screen.title: Roles & Grants
core.roles.screen.subtitle: Access roles and assignments used to decide who can work where.
core.tenancy.screen.title: Tenant Structure
core.tenancy.screen.subtitle: Organizations, scopes, memberships, and principal placement across the workspace.
core.reference-data.screen.title: Reference catalogs
core.reference-data.screen.subtitle: Manage governed business options used across the workspace.
core.audit.screen.title: Audit Trail
core.audit.screen.subtitle: Recent security and governance events recorded by the audit trail.
core.audit.summary: Exports are available from the toolbar. This view intentionally stays readable first and forensic second.
core.notifications.screen.title: Notifications & Delivery
core.notifications.screen.subtitle: In-app reminders, due dispatch state, and organization-scoped outbound email delivery.
core.api-tokens.screen.title: API Tokens
core.api-tokens.screen.subtitle: Issue and revoke API credentials linked to user principals for automation and integrations.
core.functional-actors.screen.title: Functional Directory
core.functional-actors.screen.subtitle: Functional actors, linked principals, and current ownership assignments.
core.assignments.screen.title: Functional Assignments
core.assignments.screen.subtitle: Responsibility links across actors, governed records, and current workspace scope.
core.object-access.screen.title: Object Access Matrix
core.object-access.screen.subtitle: Inspect and govern object-scoped visibility across principals, actors, and governed workspace records.
core.support.intro: This page explains the main concepts of the application, why they exist, and how they relate to each other.
core.support.navigation_model: Navigation model: most operational modules now use `list -> detail`, with `Open` as the main action from summary lists. Governance-heavy administration stays under `/admin` so dense setup work does not crowd operational screens.
core.support.index_subtitle: Every concept used in the application appears here so people can understand the workspace without learning internal architecture first.
core.support.how_to_read_heading: How to use this help page
core.support.how_to_read_copy: Read from top to bottom the first time. Start with the structural concepts, then move to access, then to operational records.
core.support.read_step_organization: Start with Organization. It is the main business boundary of the workspace.
core.support.read_step_access: Continue with Scope, Principal, Membership, and Role to understand who can work where.
core.support.read_step_records: Then read the operational concepts such as assets, controls, risks, findings, exceptions, privacy records, and continuity plans.
core.support.read_step_relations: Use the relationship map to understand how one record connects to another.
core.support.guide_subtitle: Short narrative guidance for people who need to use the system without knowing the internal model first.
core.support.reference_subtitle: Detailed reference for each concept, what it is for, and how it should be used.
core.support.relationships_subtitle: Cross-reference of how the main concepts connect to each other.
core.support.no_how_copy: This concept does not yet have step-by-step usage notes.
core.support.no_related_copy: No explicit relationships have been documented for this concept yet.
core.support.no_relationships_copy: No concept relationships have been declared yet.
This page is open in detail context and: This page is open in detail context and returns to its parent list.
Edit this record without compressing the: Edit this record without compressing the table layout.
Select an organization to focus the dash: Select an organization to focus the dashboard on one workspace.
Open the areas that usually need review,: Open the areas that usually need review, evidence, or follow-up.
No workspaces are visible for the curren: No workspaces are visible for the current access context yet.
Use this as a quick triage view before d: Use this as a quick triage view before diving into one module.
Follow assets still under lifecycle revi: Follow assets still under lifecycle review.
Check risks that remain in assessment.
Push controls waiting for evidence or ap: Push controls waiting for evidence or approval.
Keep findings and exceptions moving towa: Keep findings and exceptions moving toward closure.
Latest sensitive changes recorded by the: Latest sensitive changes recorded by the platform audit trail.
No audit activity has been recorded yet.
Governance page. Use this area to define: Governance page. Use this area to define tenant boundaries, default workspace settings, and operational scopes. Day-to-day record maintenance stays in the application workspaces, not here.
Start a new tenant boundary with its def: Start a new tenant boundary with its default locale and timezone.
Add a bounded workspace inside an existi: Add a bounded workspace inside an existing organization.
Tenant boundaries, defaults, and current: Tenant boundaries, defaults, and current workspace state.
Platform admins can change tenant metada: Platform admins can change tenant metadata here.
Update tenant identity and defaults with: Update tenant identity and defaults without squeezing the form into the table.
Name: Name
Operational perimeters bound to one orga: Operational perimeters bound to one organization.
Scope lifecycle is managed by platform a: Scope lifecycle is managed by platform administrators.
Keep the table readable and open the edi: Keep the table readable and open the editor only when you need it.
Access assignments stay visible here, bu: Access assignments stay visible here, but user access is managed from People and Access.
Use the identity screens to create membe: Use the identity screens to create memberships, assign scopes, and grant roles. This panel keeps tenancy structure and access structure clearly separated.
Module activation changes are stored in: Module activation changes are stored in :path. Module-specific setup lives in each module's own settings area.
Governance page. Enable or disable modul: Governance page. Enable or disable modules here, but keep module-specific business operations inside each module workspace.
Select an organization to configure this: Select an organization to configure this module.
Disable is blocked until dependent modul: Disable is blocked until dependent modules are disabled first.
Enable is blocked until required depende: Enable is blocked until required dependencies are enabled.
This module can be disabled now.
This module can be enabled now.
core.api-tokens.summary: API tokens are bound to one person and can optionally be restricted to one organization or scope. Token secrets are only shown once after issuance.
core.api-tokens.secret.title: Copy this token now
core.api-tokens.secret.subtitle: This value is not stored in clear text and cannot be retrieved again after leaving this page.
core.api-tokens.secret.label: Bearer token
core.api-tokens.issue.title: Issue API token
core.api-tokens.issue.subtitle: Create a new token and bind it to a user identity.
core.api-tokens.form.label: Label
core.api-tokens.form.abilities_copy: Abilities are permission keys separated by spaces or commas. If left blank, the token receives effective owner permissions for the selected context.
core.api-tokens.list.title: Issued tokens
core.api-tokens.list.subtitle: All tokens currently issued for the selected platform context.
core.api-tokens.table.empty: No API tokens issued yet in this context.
core.functional-actors.summary: Governance page. Functional profiles and accountability links live here so ownership stays separate from day-to-day record maintenance.
core.functional-actors.person_context.co: This person can be linked to one or more functional profiles for team placement and object-level accountability.
core.functional-actors.create.title: New functional profile
core.functional-actors.link.title: Link person to profile
core.functional-actors.detail.summary: Functional Profile Detail keeps linked people and responsibilities in one governance workspace. Use the profile list to browse profiles and open the one you want to manage.
core.functional-actors.detail.subtitle: :kind profile used to assign ownership and accountability across the workspace.
core.functional-actors.detail.no_linked_: No people linked to this profile yet.
core.functional-actors.detail.no_assignm: No assignments recorded for this profile.
core.functional-actors.list.title: Functional profile list
core.functional-actors.list.subtitle: Browse profiles, review context, and open one profile when you need governance editing.
core.functional-actors.recent_links.subt: People linked to functional profiles most recently.
core.functional-actors.recent_assignment: Responsibilities assigned to functional profiles across managed records.
core.roles.summary: Governance page. Define reusable role sets and grants here, then open one role or grant when you need dense editing. Operational work stays in the domain workspaces.
core.roles.role_detail.summary: Role Detail keeps permission selection and role maintenance in one governance workspace. Use the role list to browse roles and open the one you want to edit.
core.roles.category.title: Category
core.roles.edit.title: Edit role
core.roles.edit.label: Label
core.roles.grant_detail.summary: Grant Detail keeps grant maintenance in one governance workspace. Use the grant list to browse grants and open the one you want to edit.
core.roles.grant.edit.title: Edit grant
core.roles.platform_note: Platform permissions belong to /admin. Day-to-day organization responsibilities should usually be granted through operational workspace role sets, with identity roles reserved for access governance.
core.roles.create.title: New role
core.roles.create.label: Label
core.roles.assign.title: New grant
core.roles.list.roles_subtitle: Browse role sets, review category fit, and open one role when you need governance editing.
core.roles.list.grants_subtitle: Browse grants by target and context, then open one grant for governance changes.
core.notifications.no_organization: Select an organization first. SMTP delivery is stored per organization so outbound settings are never shared across tenants.
core.notifications.summary: Outbound delivery stays organization-scoped. The platform only stores the SMTP connector for the active organization and notifications keep their in-app record even if email delivery fails.
core.notifications.smtp.title: SMTP delivery
core.notifications.smtp.subtitle: Configure the outbound connector used when due notifications are dispatched for this organization.
core.notifications.smtp.enable_copy: When enabled, due notifications with a matching principal email are also sent through SMTP.
core.notifications.smtp.password_stored: Stored securely. Leave blank to keep current password.
core.notifications.smtp.password_optiona: Optional unless your provider requires it.
core.notifications.test.title: Send test email
core.notifications.test.subtitle: Verify the connector with a real principal from the active organization.
core.notifications.test.empty_people: No active principals with email addresses are available in this organization yet.
core.notifications.test.copy: The test message only proves connector health. It does not expose workspace records or customer data.
core.notifications.templates.title: Notification templates
core.notifications.templates.subtitle: Override title and body per notification type without changing plugin code.
core.notifications.templates.empty: No notification types have been discovered for this organization yet.
core.notifications.editor.title: Template editor
core.notifications.editor.subtitle: Use placeholders to adapt reminders and operational follow-up to your organization tone.
core.notifications.editor.empty: Select a notification type first to edit its template.
core.notifications.editor.enable_overrid: If disabled, the notification falls back to the title and body supplied by the core or plugin.
core.notifications.recent.title: Recent notifications
core.notifications.recent.subtitle: Latest reminder and notification records for the active organization context.
core.notifications.recent.empty: No notifications recorded for the active organization context yet.
core.reference-data.summary: Reference catalogs keep business labels consistent across the workspace. Defaults are available immediately; organization-specific overrides let admins add, rename, or retire options without editing code.
core.reference-data.scope_note: These catalogs are for business-managed vocabularies such as risk categories or policy areas. System workflow states and fixed platform enums are governed in code and are not edited here.
core.reference-data.editor.title: New managed option
core.reference-data.editor.label: Label
core.reference-data.selected.no_managed_: This organization is still using the default options for this catalog.
core.functional-assignments.title: Functional Assignments
core.functional-assignments.summary: Responsibility links across actors, governed records, and current workspace scope.
core.functional-assignments.no_workspace: No workspace view is available for this record.
core.functional-assignments.empty: No assignments were found for the current workspace context.
core.governance.summary: Use this page to inspect delegated access across linked principals, functional actors, and governed objects.
core.governance.no_organization: Select an organization first. Governance is always resolved inside one organization context.
core.governance.entrypoint.title: Access entrypoints
core.governance.entrypoint.subtitle: Use these records to understand where platform identities enter the workspace.
core.governance.boundaries.title: Delegated object boundaries
core.governance.boundaries.subtitle: Use these records to understand which governed objects remain visible and who can act on them.
core.management-reporting.screen.hero_ti: Management view across assessments, evidence, risks, and findings
core.management-reporting.screen.hero_co: Use this page to understand current delivery pressure before going into one operational module.
core.management-reporting.no_organizatio: Select an organization first. Management reporting is always resolved inside one organization context.
core.management-reporting.visibility_not: Assessments and evidence keep organization-wide records visible when a scope is selected. Risks and findings stay scope-exact and continue to respect delegated object access.
core.management-reporting.executive.subt: Use these sections to compare domain pressure without dropping into operational queues yet.
core.management-reporting.operational.su: Use these queues only after the executive summary tells you where to drill down.
core.management-reporting.section.assess: Campaign status, review results, and the linked findings load carried by current assessments.
core.management-reporting.section.eviden: Review queue, expiry attention, and validation gaps for the current workspace context.
Workflow load and residual score concentration across visible risks.
core.management-reporting.section.findin: Severity mix, overdue exposure, and remediation action pressure.
core.management-reporting.section.vendor: Vendor review load, decision posture, reassessment timing, and unresolved third-party follow-up.
core.management-reporting.attention.asse: Campaigns with active pressure, unresolved reviews, or linked findings.
core.management-reporting.attention.evid: Items with review, expiry, or validation pressure in the current context.
core.management-reporting.attention.risk: Risks with elevated residual exposure or workflow pressure.
core.management-reporting.attention.find: Findings with open actions, due dates, or unresolved remediation pressure.
core.management-reporting.attention.vend: Third parties with review follow-up, reassessment pressure, or open remediation.
core.object-access.no_organization: Select an organization first. Object access matrices are always resolved inside one organization context.
core.object-access.summary: Roles still open workspaces. This screen governs which records stay visible once a person is linked to functional actors that own or review specific objects.
core.object-access.inspect.title: Inspect principal visibility
core.object-access.inspect.subtitle: Choose a person to inspect linked actors, direct governed objects, and per-domain visibility mode.
core.object-access.no_direct_governed_ob: No direct governed objects were found for this person in the active organization.
core.object-access.matrix.title: Govern object matrix
core.object-access.matrix.subtitle: Assign or retire actor ownership and review responsibilities for a specific workspace object.
core.object-access.current_assignments.s: Select an object above to inspect the matrix.
core.object-access.no_active_assignments: No active assignments for the selected object.
